observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted. Likewise, trace the noted difference to make proper adjustment to the reciprocal accounts and effect the immediate settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair presentation of the cash position of each fund.
observations.managementAction: As per discussion during the exit conference, unreconciled balance of the Due to/Due from between this Municipality and Province revealed that the amount ₱685,073.25 is the share of the Municipality from taxes collected by the Province which were not taken in the books of the Municipality. The other balances are long outstanding balances that have been in the books long before the Municipal Accountant assumed office. However, in line with the recommendation, reconciliation between the two is set to take place. As per the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipality is just waiting for its schedule with the Province for the one-on-one reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts since the Province is prioritizing Municipality’s with material discrepancies.
observations.reason: Difficulty in the reconciliation of old accounts hindered the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts. The Municipality is waiting for schedule with the Province for one-on-one reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to stop the practice of charging honoraria and wages of Job Order personnel out of savings from the fund established for specific purpose. Also require the Accountant to ensure that the necessary documentation for payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC and TWG are submitted and comply with allowed fund sources provided in DBM Circular No. 2007-3.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee have for several years complied with all the requirements of the bidding process. The documents mentioned were specifically checked by each member of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC). Copies of the above-mentioned lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team and found complete. The Municipality specified that the BAC was instructed by the PRDP to follow GPPB Resolution No. 06-2010. The Municipality also noted that some honoraria were charged out of savings for CY 2017 which were incurred prior to receipt by the Agency of the Audit Observation issued in CY 2016.
observations.reason: There were still honoraria and wages charged from funds other than the sources prescribed by DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on CY 2017. Some charges were incurred prior to the receipt of the Audit Observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and prepare and submit financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to facilitate evaluation of operations and provide management with vital information relative to assets, liabilities, equity and income generated by each special account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the justification on the status and prepared financial statements for economic enterprises as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Accounts were maintained and financial statements for economic enterprises were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to limit the use of the motor vehicles to disaster management related activities and install appropriate controls relative to fuel consumption; ensure submission of required reports on utilization of DRRMF to the Auditor; limit disbursements out of the fund to those pertaining to disaster risk management as provided by law and that proper supporting documents be submitted; and that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile amounts and refund where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office commented on the actions and provided some supporting schedules. The Municipality acknowledged the recommendations and will comply. The last report received by the Audit Team was up to the 2nd Quarter of 2017 only.
observations.reason: There were still expenses which were not priority projects and activities provided by RA 10121 and non-submission or late submission of reports hindered verification. The last report received by the Audit Team was up to the 2nd Quarter of 2017 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to monitor and require barangays to implement MRFs; establish partnerships and orient constituents; enact necessary ordinances and resolutions; provide trainings; and review the 10-year SWM Plan every two years and ensure implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality assured that a Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperative/Association will be established; additional barangays have established MRFs and more pledged to establish facilities; the Municipality plans to procure a vehicle intended for garbage collection and to coordinate for proper trainings for collectors and barangay officials.
observations.reason: There are still MRFs which are not functional/not-operational and no resolutions or ordinances issued for 2017. Some remedial actions are planned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer justify reasons why some projects listed in the CY 2016 20% Development Plan are not implemented despite availability of funds; the Municipal Engineer to report regularly on project status for proper monitoring; and comply strictly with Section 5 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer submitted justification together with the status of projects and monitoring reports and implemented some projects; quarterly status reports were submitted. The Municipal Engineer submitted updates and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer provided the justification.
observations.reason: There were lapses on utilization and implementation of the 20% Development Fund for CY 2017; some projects still had delays and transactions noted during audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Head of the Local School Board ensure that SEF projects and disbursements are strictly used for allowable purposes as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 and Section 272 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Education and related offices explained reasons for non-implementation including transfers of school heads, receipts of materials from DepEd, and projects carried over to CY 2018. Some projects were implemented late and liquidations were submitted.
observations.reason: Insufficiency of the budgeted amounts and transfers of school heads caused non-implementation in 2017; some late liquidations remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that membership dues and contributions of elected officials and institution are paid in accordance with RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 and to avoid charging municipal funds for non-allowable expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that dues were paid and that they will comply with regulations in the future.
observations.reason: No further dues were paid in 2017 beyond those provided in RA 7160; compliance undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to ensure that any adjustments to the GAD Plan and Budget be endorsed to DILG and ensure compliance with reporting and monitoring requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the justification and the GAD Focal Point System revised documents; some validation was undertaken with PPDO and DILG and the Municipality will adhere to recommendations.
observations.reason: There were still inconsistencies noted between GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report and differences in amounts used for reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to ensure accounts are properly kept, disbursements and supporting documents are timely submitted, that cash advances be used only by authorized persons and that liquidation reports be submitted immediately.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to submit missing Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidation Reports and to ensure compliance with rules on cash advances and proper accounting. Some missing documents were submitted after the audit.
observations.reason: Not all lacking documents were submitted at time of audit; some liquidation reports remained unsubmitted though Municipal Accountant committed to submit them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Sanggunian include among top priorities the construction of a building for safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the Municipality with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years.
observations.managementAction: The management assured that an office beside the Municipal Accounting Office will be provided to the assigned Auditors immediately after the Municipal DRRM Office personnel vacate the premises. As to the storage room of documents, the Municipality will provide budget for the construction/improvement of the Municipal building which will provide a space for such documents.
observations.reason: An office was to be provided beside the Municipal Accounting Office and plans made for storage improvements; immediate measures undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned officials to expedite settlement/closure of unresolved disallowances, suspensions and charges and coordinate with COA for proper disposition.
observations.managementAction: No specific settlement was recorded during the year; records show balances remained. Management acknowledged and will act to settle outstanding disallowances/charges.
observations.reason: No settlements made during the year; balances remained outstanding and reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended continuous monitoring and timely remittance of withheld taxes and ensure accurate reporting and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continues to comply with withholding taxes and remittances and will strengthen monitoring and reconciliation procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned/authorized LGU officials draw cash advance for the Confidential and Intelligence Expenses and liquidate immediately after the intended purpose it was drawn has been completed by transmitting the liquidation report and all supporting documents to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU), for audit.
observations.reason: Confidential and Intelligence Expenses were noted through direct disbursement of fund instead of thru cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete and actual physical count of all LGU’s properties, prepare an inventory report thereon and submit the same to the Audit Team as required by the aforementioned regulation. Also, we recommended that the Management review the charging of expenses made particularly those which were capitalized despite being not directly attributable to a PPE item and effect appropriate adjustments to arrive at the correct balance of PPE account. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the unrecorded assets arising from Trust Fund.

We recommended that the management require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the accounting of supplies and materials, recognize the Inventory account for purchases out of Trust Fund and subsequently record the corresponding Expense account upon its distribution to end-users to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements within ten (10) days upon receipt at the end of each month  in order to present a reliable balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account.  Likewise, the cash account balance in the cashbook and its ledger balance should be regularly reconciled.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor immediately instruct the Municipal Accountant coordinate closely with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made to reflect the correct balances of reciprocal accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to stop the practice of charging honoraria out of the savings of the Approved Budget for the Contract and the contract cost and instead comply with the allowed fund sources provided in the DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 as likewise cited in GPPB Resolution No. 18-2012.
We also recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly compliance with the submission of the required documentation for payment of honorarium to the members of the BAC and TWG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant  to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and  prepare and submit  financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I  to facilitate evaluation of operations and provide management with vital information relative to assets, liabilities, equity and income generated by each special account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

the concerned Municipal officials to ensure that disbursements out of the fund are those that only pertain to disaster risk and management activities provided by RA 10121 and that each disbursement is supported with proper attachments. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the submission of the lacking supporting documents.

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions pertaining to cash advance are reported in the Schedule of Cash Advances. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and MDRRMO reconcile the difference per summary list provided and the actual amount of financial assistance released to the affected families of Typhoon “Nina” and submit a copy of the payroll and liquidation of cash advance pertaining to that.

the MDRRMO limit the use of the motor vehicles to disaster management related activities and install appropriate controls relative to fuel consumption.

the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO prepare and  submit the Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan be reviewed and updated with budget allocation for implementation as required under Section 16 of R.A. 9003 for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

Moreover, to ensure efficiency and effectiveness of solid waste management program the following are recommended:
Monitor and require Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee in all barangays to submit SWM monthly status reports. The use of the Materials Recovery Facility be strictly enforced in the barangay level to address the problem on the collection and disposal of wastes per household.
Constituents shall be oriented and guided on the proper waste segregation, recycling and disposal of wastes and be made aware of the corresponding sanctions/penalties for non-compliance thereof.
Management enact resolutions and   ordinances   aligned   with   the Municipality’s solid waste management plan and ensure implementation  of  the  enacted  resolutions/ordinances by informing  the  community  of  its provisions and imposing appropriate fines to violators;
Partnership and linkages with schools and private sector be  established to  assist  in  the  implementation  of SWM programs.   Provisions  of receptacles properly labeled and  strategically  placed  in conspicuous place must be observed;
Integration of SWM orientation upon renewal/application  of  mayor’s permit and business licenses as part of their strategies to  ensure  the  viability and effective implementation of solid waste management program in the entire municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

The Municipal Planning and Development Officer to justify the reason why some of the projects listed in the CY 2016 20% Development Plan are not implemented in spite availability of funds. Likewise, evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects to ensure delivery of its intended services to the public.
The Municipal Administrator to require the Municipal Engineer/Project Engineer to report regularly on the status of the projects for proper monitoring and evaluation of the efficiency of the implementation.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 5 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) N0. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the community optimally without delay.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board officials prioritize and implement projects and activities chargeable against Special Education Fund to its optimal level to address the needs of the school children for quality education as provided for under Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG. Likewise, the Requisitioning Office should disclose in the Purchase Request/Canvass complete details/specifications of the items except for brand names to determine the lowest responsive offer.

Likewise, the Local School Board ensures that the fund should be only expended for projects chargeable against Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563April 22, 2016.  Contributions and elective officials expenses related to leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive warrant the GAD Focal Point System of the Municipality to ensure that any changes in the GAD Plan and Budget is endorsed to DILG for its review and approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and to strictly adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal GSO perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts including prior years’ acquisitions in lump sum amount and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Differences, if any, should be identified and traced whether these are missing or unserviceable.  

Likewise, the Municipal GSO initiate the immediate disposal of those unserviceable properties in accordance with Sections 162 to 168 of the aforementioned rules and regulations prescribed per COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvaged value.
observations.managementAction: The LCE created the Awards Committee on Disposal of Unserviceable properties to facilitate the auction of said properties.
observations.reason:
Non-Traceability of the physical/actual existence of some units in the accounting records of the PPE









Some of the unserviceable properties cannot be assessed as to value due to incomplete records.

Assessor’s Office did a regular update on the assessment record and classification and furnish the Treasurer’s Office of the assessment roll monthly

For year 2016, Treasurer’s Office provide certified list of taxpayers for setting up of receivables




Municipal Accountant set up tax receivables based on the certified list provided by the Municipal Treasurer

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entry to transfer the CY 2015 remaining unexpended balance of ₱1,000,000.00  to Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books and maintain the required subsidiary ledger.


Likewise, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day at the end of each month, for review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the concerned Municipal officials to require the submission of the lacking documents.  Ensure the completeness of supporting documents for claims against government funds as required in Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor already submitted the lacking documents that are applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials concerned ensure that drug products requisitioned fall within and conform with the PNDF, Volume I, current edition to avoid suspension/disallowance in the future. Also, require that all procurement of medicines and other drugs be supported with certifications to that effect. For drugs and medicines not listed in PNDF Volume I, submit a written request with corresponding justification addressed to the Head of the National Drug Policy Staff who may approve or disapprove the request.
observations.managementAction: No drugs/medicines requisition was purchased outside the PNDF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to accountable officers who failed to effect prompt liquidation of their cash advances. Otherwise, the accountable officers shall be held criminally liable for failure to settle their accounts.
observations.managementAction: Close monitoring of cash advances of municipal & issuance of demand letter if necessary to employees to ensure liquidation of the same within the reglementary period which is 30 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer remit intact all taxes withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue within the prescribed period in compliance with the revenue regulation and to provide the national government of the funds for its operation and development projects. Charge interest and penalties for late payment of taxes to the personal account of the official/employee who remiss his/her duty.
observations.managementAction: For all the taxes withheld within the year 2016, the office concerned made sure that those were remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 as well as DENR Administrative Order (DAO) No. 9, Series of 2006 particularly on the immediate and safe closure of LGU’s existing controlled dumpsite and put up or convert the site into a sanitary landfill as an alternative.
observations.managementAction: This LGU effect the closure of the dumpsite. In fact they have negotiated with Bauan Solid Waste Waste Management Incorporated for the disposal of waste.

The LGU incorporated in the 2016 Annual Budget programs & projects related to GAD which meet the threshold of 5% of the total annual budget.

Review of the GFPS structure is ongoing.











the office/official concerned made sure that the next GAD Plan & budget are being reviewed  and approved by the DILG Regional Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipality terminate/close immediately all  accounts with the Philippine Veterans Bank  and transfer all  funds with the nearest  LBP or  DBP  branch in compliance   with Department of Finance Order No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015.  Otherwise, secure prior approval from the Department  of  Finance (DOF) /Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF), for the  exception.
observations.managementAction: In July 2016, the management opened Current Account for SEF and Trust Fund in DBP with initial deposit of ₱500,000 and ₱5,000,000.00, respectively. Minimal deposits in PVB are being maintained in PVB.
observations.reason: According to Republict Act enacted by the late Pres. Cory Aquino Philippine Veterans Bank is an authorized depository bank of government funds.
